Antiviral therapy is reserved for cases of varicella-zoster and herpes simplex infection.
Most people develop antibodies to the chicken pox virus, referred to as varicella.
The chickenpox virus (called varicella zoster virus) remains inactive within the nervous system but may show up as shingles in about 20 percent of people.
